### Mount Lewotobi Laki-Laki, on Flores Island, East Nusa Tenggara Province
FCDO advises against all travel to within 7km of the crater of Mount Lewotobi Laki-Laki on Flores Island, East Nusa Tenggara province.
Mount Lewotobi Laki-Laki erupted on 17 June 2025.
### Mount Sinabung, Karo Regency, North Sumatra
FCDO advises against all travel to within 5km of the crater of Mount Sinabung in Karo Regency, North Sumatra.
Mount Sinabung erupted in 2021, sending a large column of smoke and ash into the sky.
### Mount Marapi, West Sumatra
FCDO advises against all travel to within 3km of the crater of Mount Marapi in West Sumatra.
Mount Marapi erupted in December 2023.
### Mount Semeru, Lumajang Regency, East Java
FCDO advises against all travel to:
within 5 km of the crater of Mount Semeru in Lumajang Regency, East Java
the south-east area of Mount Semeru along the Besuk Kobokan river, approximately 13km from the crater, and 500m from any Besuk Kobokan riverbank
Mount Semeru erupted in 2022, emitting a 1,500m (4,921ft) high ash cloud.
Indonesian authorities have set the alert level to 4 (of 4), warning of the potential for hot pyroclastic flows and lava flows along rivers, tributaries and valleys around Mount Semeru.
### Mount Ruang, Northern Sulawesi
FCDO advises against all travel to within 7km of the crater of Mount Ruang in Northern Sulawesi.
Mount Ruang erupted on 16 April 2024.
### Mount Ibu, North Maluku Province
FCDO advises against all travel to within 7km of the crater of Mount Ibu in North Maluku Province.
Mount Ibu erupted on 6 June 2024.
You can get a [30-day visa on arrival (VOA)](https://evisa.imigrasi.go.id/) for:
The VOA costs 500,000 Indonesian rupiah, payable in cash or by card.
Most of the [main airports and ports](https://kemlu.go.id/london/en/news/18631/visa-on-arrival-voa-for-foreign-travellers) issue VOAs.
You can get a [30-day visa on arrival (VOA)](https://evisa.imigrasi.go.id/) for:
Make sure your visa is approved before arriving in Indonesia, otherwise you may only be eligible for a 30-day visa on arrival.
The 30-day visa can be extended once, for another 30 days, at any immigration office within Indonesia.
You can get a [30-day visa on arrival (VOA)](https://evisa.imigrasi.go.id/) for:
You will find options to apply for a multiple-entry visa or a 60-day visa.
If you're a KITAS holder with an expired stay permit, you can apply for an extension from outside Indonesia through a sponsor.

Before travelling to Indonesia, fill out the online customs and health declaration on the All Indonesia arrival card website or app within 72 hours of arrival.
To enter Indonesia, you must have a certificate to prove you've had a yellow fever vaccination if you're coming from a country listed as a transmission risk.
This information is for people travelling on a full ['British citizen' passport](https://www.gov.uk/types-of-british-nationality) from the UK.
If you choose to travel, research your destinations and [get appropriate travel insurance](https://www.gov.uk/guidance/foreign-travel-insurance).
Contact your insurance company quickly if you're referred to a medical facility for treatment.
you have appropriate travel insurance for local treatment or unexpected medical evacuation

make sure you're covered by insurance

Contact your travel provider and your insurer if you are involved in a serious incident or emergency abroad.
Insurance should cover your itinerary, planned activities and expenses in an emergency.

The Bali provincial government has introduced a tourist levy of 150,000 Indonesian rupiah (approximately £8) for all foreign tourists arriving in Bali.
The Bali provincial government has introduced a tourist levy of 150,000 Indonesian rupiah (approximately £8) for all foreign tourists arriving in Bali.
For some international flights departing Indonesia, airport departure tax might not be included in the price of the ticket.
In Aceh, the consumption, production and distribution of alcohol are all illegal under Sharia law.

Ngurah Rai International Airport is closed for the entire day.

FCDO provides advice about risks of travel to help you make informed decisions.

Read more advice for LGBT+ travellers.
- see [general advice for LGBT+ travellers](https://www.gov.uk/guidance/lesbian-gay-bisexual-and-transgender-foreign-travel-advice)
Across Indonesia, there have been reports of police arresting individuals at LGBT+ events.
Some laws and regulations, including those related to pornography and prostitution, have been applied in a way that discriminates against the LGBT+ community.
- read about [safety for solo and independent travel](https://www.gov.uk/guidance/solo-and-independent-travel)
Punishment following a Sharia court process is public caning.
Punishment following a Sharia court process includes public caning, a prison sentence or a fine payable in gold.
Sharia law is in force in Aceh.
There have been reports of Sharia (religious) police harassing foreigners.
You should confirm the appropriate visa for your visit at the [Indonesian immigration website](https://www.imigrasi.go.id/wna/permohonan-visa-republik-indonesia).
Use the Indonesian immigration website to get or extend your visa.
If your visa will expire while you are in hospital or in detention, contact Indonesian Immigration before it expires to avoid any overstay fines.
To enter Indonesia, your passport must have an 'expiry date' at least 6 months after the date you arrive and have at least 2 blank pages.
For some international flights departing Indonesia, airport departure tax might not be included in the price of the ticket.
Airport tax is included in the cost of all domestic flights within Indonesia.
Reported cases of rape and sexual assault are high in Bali and Lombok.
Reported cases of rape and sexual assault are high in Bali and Lombok.
There have been reports of drink spiking linked to sexual assault in Bali, Lombok and the Gili Islands.
Be aware of the risk of street crime such as bag-snatching and pickpocketing, particularly in busy tourist areas.
There are often reports of bag-snatching in Bali.
Be aware of the risk of street crime such as bag-snatching and pickpocketing, particularly in busy tourist areas.
Credit card fraud is common.
Criminals sometimes place a fake telephone number on ATMs advising customers to report problems so they can fraudulently ask for card details.
Ramadan is a holy month for Muslims.
Ramadan in Indonesia is from 19 February to 20 March 2026.
You must always carry your passport or stay permit (such as KITAS or KITAP).
You must always carry your passport or stay permit (such as KITAS or KITAP).
Beware of visa scams by fake visa agents who, having taken your money, may fail to provide a visa or supply the wrong visa.
Visa scams are increasing in Indonesia.
This could result in you overstaying and getting a fine of 1 million rupiah a day, plus possible deportation and a re-entry ban.
Online visas approved after entry are not valid and may result in overstaying penalties.
Make sure you extend your visa within the initial 30 days to avoid an overstay fine of 1 million rupiah a day.
The cost of overstaying your visa is one million rupiah a day.
If your visa will expire while you are in hospital or in detention, contact Indonesian Immigration before it expires to avoid any overstay fines.
Avoid all protests, demonstrations and student and political rallies as they can become violent without warning.
Political tensions have also led to violent demonstrations in cities across Papua, which have turned violent at short notice.
The political situation in Indonesia is stable.
elections, internal and external developments, including in the Middle East, can trigger protests or unrest.
Be alert to the risk of politically motivated violence.
The political situation in Central Sulawesi Province is unsettled.
Be alert to the risk of politically motivated violence.
Unlicensed money changers may short-change you.
Only use licensed currency exchanges, identified by a Bank of Indonesia logo and a QR code.
The province of Aceh enforces Sharia law.
Sharia law is in force in Aceh.
In the province of Aceh gambling is illegal under Sharia law.
In the province of Aceh, extra-marital sex is illegal under Sharia law and punishable by public caning after a Sharia court process.
Nyepi (Balinese New Year)
Balinese New Year ('Nyepi') is on 19 March 2026.
During this time, local custom requires that all people in Bali observe a day of silence by staying indoors, turning off lights, and making no noise.

The political situation in Central Sulawesi Province is unsettled.
Take particular care in Palu, Poso and Tentena.
This conflict is predominantly in the Sigi, Parigi Moutong and Poso regions.
The Maluku Islands have experienced unrest and violence between different religious and tribal groups.
Armed Papuan separatist groups are fighting for independence from Indonesia in the region of Papua.
The Indonesian government has a heavy security presence throughout the region especially in Highland Papua and Central Papua provinces and along the border with Papua New Guinea.
a New Zealand national was kidnapped by a separatist group in Nduga, Highland Papua Province
Political tensions have also led to violent demonstrations in cities across Papua, which have turned violent at short notice.
Papua and West Papua have been reorganised into 6 provinces:
The Indonesian government has a heavy security presence throughout the region especially in Highland Papua and Central Papua provinces and along the border with Papua New Guinea.
There are regular armed clashes between Papuan separatist groups (known as the TPNPB-OPM) and government security forces, mainly in the regions of Highland Papua and Central Papua.
In 2020, a New Zealand national was shot dead and a number of Indonesian nationals were wounded in a mining facility in Highland Papua Province.
a New Zealand national was kidnapped by a separatist group in Nduga, Highland Papua Province

There have been several instances of Papuan separatists attacking commercial aircraft that service remote communities from small local airfields, especially in Highland Papua and Central Papua provinces.
The security situation in Highland Papua and Central Papua provinces is unpredictable and can change quickly.

Same-sex sexual activity is illegal in the province of Aceh under Sharia law.
Indonesia's new criminal code imposes penalties for cohabitation and sex outside of marriage if certain members of either party's immediate family file a complaint.
You cannot drive using your UK driving licence.
You can drive using an International Driving Permit (IDP) issued by the UK.

Air quality in Indonesia's major cities can reach levels classed as 'unhealthy for sensitive groups' or 'unhealthy'.
Ash plumes and pollutants from volcanoes can affect air quality and have an impact on health.
The fires, mostly on Sumatra and Borneo, generate a widespread haze that affects air quality throughout the country.
The haze can cause disruption to local and regional air travel, and the air pollution has an impact on public health.
The haze can cause disruption to local and regional air travel, and the air pollution has an impact on public health.
See [what health risks you'll face in Indonesia](https://travelhealthpro.org.uk/country/106/indonesia), including: - poor air quality - Zika virus - malaria - dengue

Follow and monitor local and international weather updates. [BMKG - Meteorology, Climatology, and Geophysics Agency](https://www.bmkg.go.id/) can be translated into English.
Follow and monitor local and international weather updates. [BMKG - Meteorology, Climatology, and Geophysics Agency](https://www.bmkg.go.id/) can be translated into English.
You can also use the BMKG Multi-Hazard Early Warning System app.
The [Indonesia Tsunami Early Warning Centre](http://rtsp.bmkg.go.id/publicbull.php) issues warnings when a potential tsunami with significant impact is imminent or expected.
The US Federal Emergency Management Agency has advice about what to do before, during and after an [earthquake](http://www.ready.gov/earthquakes) or [tsunami](http://www.ready.gov/tsunamis).
The main toll road to Soekarno-Hatta International Airport and other major roads in Jakarta can be affected by flooding.
Indonesian authorities have raised the volcano status alert to the highest level (level 4), and have warned of potential mud and debris flows within the exclusion zone.
Check news reports and [updates on volcanic activity](https://magma.esdm.go.id/v1/gunung-api/laporan) (in Bahasa language) before travelling to areas that are prone to volcanic activity.
Indonesia regularly experiences natural disasters such as severe weather, floods, landslides, volcanic eruptions, earthquakes and tsunamis.

There are landslides in mountainous and remote areas, but also in some urban areas/cities, especially Jakarta, and the island of Bali, can suffer severe localised flooding resulting in major traffic congestion and power outages.
Flash floods and widespread flooding can occur across Indonesia, damaging infrastructure, and causing injury and loss of life.
If a major earthquake or landslide occurs close to shore, follow the instructions of local authorities, bearing in mind that a tsunami could arrive within minutes.
Indonesia regularly experiences natural disasters such as severe weather, floods, landslides, volcanic eruptions, earthquakes and tsunamis.
Earthquakes happen regularly.
Wildfires in Indonesia's forests are most severe during the dry season, usually from April to September.
During the dry season from May to November, widespread wildfires cause smoke haze, which drifts across parts of Indonesia, particularly Riau Islands, central Sumatra and Kalimantan.
The fires, mostly on Sumatra and Borneo, generate a widespread haze that affects air quality throughout the country.

Before driving, riding or hiring any type of vehicle, ensure that you have the appropriate licence to do so.
Traffic discipline is very poor and there has been a large increase in moped and motorbike accidents – some fatal – in Bali.
Travelling by boat or ferry can be dangerous as the sea conditions can change rapidly.
Vessels can be crowded and overloaded and are often poorly maintained.
There is a high level of piracy and armed robbery against ships in and around the Sulu and Celebes seas and in the Malacca Straits.
There is a high threat of terrorist attack globally affecting UK interests and British nationals, including from groups and individuals who view the UK and British nationals as targets.
Terrorists are likely to try to carry out attacks in Indonesia.
Diving school standards are not always as high as in the UK.
